nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lovel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Lovell
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wolf cub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Compare Love and Low .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Lovel was born about 1193, in England, United Kingdom, her father, William Lovel, was 60 and her mother, Isabel de Montague, was 40. She married Adam de Chetwynd in 1194, in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died about 1286, in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Lovel est née environ 1193, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Lovel, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Montague, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Adam de Chetwynd en 1194, à Chetwynd, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1286, à Chetwynd, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 95 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Lovel um 1193 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Lovel 60 und ihre Mutter Isabel de Montague 40. Sie hat 1194 in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Adam de Chetwynd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb um 1286 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Lovel è nata il circa 1193 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Lovel, aveva 60 anni e sua madre, Isabel de Montague, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Adam de Chetwynd nel 1194, nel Chetwynd, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì circa 1286, nel Chetwynd, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 95 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Lovel(이)가 대략 1193년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Lovel(은)는 60세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Montague(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1194년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쳇w이드에서 Adam de Chetwynd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1286년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쳇w이드에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Lovel родилась около 1193 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Lovel было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Montague, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam de Chetwynd в 1194 г. в местности четвинд, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла около 1286 г. в местности четвинд, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 95 лет.
